name: change-a-date
goal: Reschedule the Discovery task about a week later by dragging its bar to the right.

steps.note: First-time user moves the pointer over the Discovery bar to see what it affords (SR-IXC-13): is a move grip, resize end handles, or a drag cursor shown on hover?
Click the bar to select the task I want to reschedule.
Selected state: does the TASK app bar appear docked below, and are direct-manipulation affordances (move grip / resize handles) visible on the bar so I know I can drag it (SR-IXC-13)?

Grab the bar body and drag it about 5 days to the right. Expect a floating date pill under the pointer updating live (SR-IXC-03), the value snapping to whole days (SR-IXC-12), and the drag staying inside the chart bounds (SR-IXC-11).
Result: did the bar move about 5 days and the start/end dates update? The date change must have been made purely by dragging, with no numeric stepper (SR-IXC-01, SR-IXC-02).
Press Esc to deselect and return the app bar and cursor to the default/component context (SR-IXC-09, SR-IXC-10).
Confirm the surface returned to a neutral default state after Esc (no sticky move cursor or contextual mode).
